topic.name: Historical Significance of Locations
topic.description: The documents discuss the historical importance and development of various cities and landmarks in the United States and Scotland.

Document 1: Dirleton Castle is a medieval fortress in the village of Dirleton, East Lothian, Scotland.
The oldest parts of the castle date to the 13th century, and it was abandoned by the end of the 17th century.
Document 2: African Americans in Omaha, Nebraska are central to the development and growth of the 43rd largest city in the United States.
In 1894 black residents of Omaha organized the first fair in the United States for African-American exhibitors and attendees.
Document 3: Harrisburg is a city in and the county seat of Saline County, Illinois, United States.
The city was one of the leading bituminous coal mining distribution hubs of the American Midwest between 1900 and 1937.

topic.name: Fungi and Mycology
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of fungi, their characteristics, taxonomy, and geographical distribution.

Document 1: Suillellus amygdalinus is a fungus of the bolete family found in western North America.
The edibility of the mushroom is not known with certainty, it may be poisonous.
Document 3: Tylopilus tabacinus is a species of bolete fungus in the Boletaceae family.
It is a mycorrhizal species, and associates with oak and beech trees.

topic.name: Music Industry Releases
topic.description: The documents discuss various music releases, including singles and video albums, by notable artists and their commercial reception.

Document 1: 'Now That You Got It' is a song by American singer and songwriter Gwen Stefani from her second solo studio album, The Sweet Escape (2006).
The song's music video was directed by The Saline Project and was shot in Puerto Rico and Jamaica.
Document 2: (sic) nesses is the fourth video album by American metal band Slipknot, released on September 28, 2010.
The album peaked at number one on four charts: the Australian Top 40 Music DVD Chart, the Finland Top10 Music DVDs, the UK Top10 Music DVD Chart, and the US Top Music Video Chart.
Document 3: 'Sale el Sol' is a song recorded by Colombian singer-songwriter Shakira for her ninth studio album of the same name.
Critical reception towards the song was positive, with many critics praising its composition and placement as the opening track of the album.

topic.name: Military and Aerospace Technology
topic.description: This topic encompasses advancements in military aircraft and naval vessels, focusing on design, operational capabilities, and technological innovations.

Document 3: The Queen Elizabeth class is a class of two supercarriers currently under construction for the Royal Navy
The first, HMS Queen Elizabeth, was named on 4 July 2014, with her ship commissioning planned for 2017.

topic.name: Professional Sports Figures
topic.description: This topic encompasses notable athletes and their careers in professional sports, particularly in baseball and basketball.

Document 1: David Falk is an American sports agent who primarily works with basketball players in the National Basketball Association.
Falk is generally considered to be the most influential player agent the NBA has seen.
Document 2: Michael Pineda is a Dominican professional baseball starting pitcher for the New York Yankees of Major League Baseball.
Pineda was named an All-Star in his rookie season.

Document 3: Manny Ramírez is a Dominican-American professional baseball outfielder who played in Major League Baseball for parts of 19 seasons.
Ramírez is recognized for having had great batting skill and power.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of transportation infrastructure, including highways and railways, and their historical development and significance in different regions.

Document 1: Ontario Highway 406 is a 400-series highway in Ontario.
Construction of Highway 406 began in 1963.
Document 2: Rail transport in Victoria, Australia, is provided by a number of railway operators.
Passenger services today are operated by Metro Trains Melbourne in suburban Melbourne.
Document 3: Whitechapel Road is a major arterial road in Whitechapel, Tower Hamlets.
The road is part of the historic Roman Road from London to Colchester.

topic.name: American Political Figures
topic.description: This cluster discusses notable American politicians, their backgrounds, political careers, and contributions to the United States.

Document 1: Rand Paul has served in the United States Senate as a member of the Republican Party representing Kentucky.
Paul has described himself as a Constitutional conservative and a supporter of the Tea Party movement.
Document 2: George W. Bush served as the 43rd President of the United States from 2001 to 2009.
He is the second president to have been the son of a former president.
Document 3: Abraham Lincoln was the 16th President of the United States, serving from March 1861 until his assassination in April 1865.
Lincoln led the United States through its Civil War.

topic.name: Naval Vessels in Warfare
topic.description: The documents discuss various naval vessels and their roles during significant historical conflicts, particularly World War II and earlier naval engagements.

Document 1: German submarine U-64 was a Type IXB U-boat of Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine during World War II.
U-64 had a very short career and sank no enemy vessels.
Document 2: HMS Amazon was a frigate of the Royal Navy.
She served during the French Revolutionary and Napoleonic Wars under several notable naval commanders.
Document 3: Molotov was a Project 26bis Kirov-class cruiser of the Soviet Navy that served during World War II.
She supported Soviet troops during the Siege of Sevastopol.

topic.name: Sports Events and Rivalries
topic.description: This cluster discusses notable sports events and the rivalries associated with them, highlighting specific competitions and athletes.

Document 1: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge.
The rivalry is a major point of honour between the two universities.
Document 2: Amber Merritt represented Australia at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in London, where she won a silver medal.
She has played for the Gliders in a number of international series including the 2010 U23 World Championship.

topic.name: Michigan Highways

topic.name: Hurricanes in the Atlantic and Pacific
topic.description: This topic encompasses the formation, impact, and meteorological history of significant hurricanes in both the Atlantic and Pacific regions.

Document 1: Hurricane Kyle was the eleventh tropical storm and sixth hurricane of the 2008 Atlantic hurricane season.
It made landfall in Nova Scotia as a Category 1 hurricane late on September 28.
Document 2: The 1909 Greater Antilles hurricane was a rare, late-season tropical cyclone that caused extensive damage and loss of life in Jamaica and Haiti.
The storm made landfall in northwest Haiti with winds of 85 mph (140 km/h).
Document 3: Hurricane Jimena was the tenth named storm and second hurricane of the 2003 Pacific hurricane season.
Jimena brushed past the Hawaiian Islands before becoming a tropical depression.

Document 3: Ethan Allen was an American Revolutionary War patriot.
